text:
![](https://assets.abnamro.com/api/public/content/shutterstock-1675867219-landscape-4x1.jpg?t=h440)
Cybersecurity for small businesses is crucial to retain customers. [Report](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/rapporten/index.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). 11/06/2024 • 3 minutes read. [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html). The European NIS2 law, the successor to the earlier Network and Information Systems Directive (NIS), forces organisations to take a critical look at the cyber resilience of suppliers and customers.

metadata.source_title: Cybersecurity for small businesses is crucial to retain customers
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Wet- en regelgeving
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: With NIS2 arriving, small businesses will face critical questions from the supply chain. This has not fully sunk in yet. Read more.
metadata.parent_text:
![](https://assets.abnamro.com/api/public/content/shutterstock-1675867219-landscape-4x1.jpg?t=h440)
Cybersecurity for small businesses is crucial to retain customers. [Report](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/rapporten/index.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). 11/06/2024 • 3 minutes read. [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html). The European NIS2 law, the successor to the earlier Network and Information Systems Directive (NIS), forces organisations to take a critical look at the cyber resilience of suppliers and customers. This means smaller businesses, which usually do not fall directly under the law, will also face critical questions from the chain.

[Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html). The European NIS2 law, the successor to the earlier Network and Information Systems Directive (NIS), forces organisations to take a critical look at the cyber resilience of suppliers and customers. This means smaller businesses, which usually do not fall directly under the law, will also face critical questions from the chain.

text: This awareness has not yet fully sunk in.

metadata.source_title: Cybersecurity for small businesses is crucial to retain customers
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Wet- en regelgeving
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: With NIS2 arriving, small businesses will face critical questions from the supply chain. This has not fully sunk in yet. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: This awareness has not yet fully sunk in.

text: The new law, which takes effect from October 2024, stipulates, among other things, that companies must also map cyber risks from their own supply chain, and that agreements on cybersecurity must be laid down contractually with these chain partners. According to CEO Maarten Roerink of cybersecurity service provider MMOX, this is no luxury. “If our customers are hacked, it very often happens indirectly.

metadata.source_title: Cybersecurity for small businesses is crucial to retain customers
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Wet- en regelgeving
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: With NIS2 arriving, small businesses will face critical questions from the supply chain. This has not fully sunk in yet. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: The new law, which takes effect from October 2024, stipulates, among other things, that companies must also map cyber risks from their own supply chain, and that agreements on cybersecurity must be laid down contractually with these chain partners. According to CEO Maarten Roerink of cybersecurity service provider MMOX, this is no luxury. “If our customers are hacked, it very often happens indirectly. Sometimes they are the target themselves, and sometimes vulnerabilities in their networks are used to penetrate the networks and data of partners.

Sometimes they are the target themselves, and sometimes vulnerabilities in their networks are used to penetrate the networks and data of partners.

metadata.source_title: Cybersecurity for small businesses is crucial to retain customers
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Wet- en regelgeving
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: With NIS2 arriving, small businesses will face critical questions from the supply chain. This has not fully sunk in yet. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: The new law, which takes effect from October 2024, stipulates, among other things, that companies must also map cyber risks from their own supply chain, and that agreements on cybersecurity must be laid down contractually with these chain partners. According to CEO Maarten Roerink of cybersecurity service provider MMOX, this is no luxury. “If our customers are hacked, it very often happens indirectly. Sometimes they are the target themselves, and sometimes vulnerabilities in their networks are used to penetrate the networks and data of partners.

text: The age-old question ‘what is there to get from my company?’ is no longer relevant.”. A survey by ABN AMRO and research agency MWM2 among 895 organisations shows that almost three-quarters (72 per cent) of respondents from large companies already question their own customers, suppliers and partners about this “very regularly” or “regularly”.

metadata.source_title: Cybersecurity for small businesses is crucial to retain customers
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Wet- en regelgeving
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: With NIS2 arriving, small businesses will face critical questions from the supply chain. This has not fully sunk in yet. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: The age-old question ‘what is there to get from my company?’ is no longer relevant.”. A survey by ABN AMRO and research agency MWM2 among 895 organisations shows that almost three-quarters (72 per cent) of respondents from large companies already question their own customers, suppliers and partners about this “very regularly” or “regularly”. Of them, 66 per cent say they also receive such questions from their chain partners. Among respondents from small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), these percentages are lower, at 52 and 37 per cent respectively. The extent to which concrete agreements are made with chain partners also differs by company size. Of respondents from large companies, 75 per cent do this, compared with 56 per cent of SMEs. Customers will set requirements. Even so, the broad chain responsibility is a reality that smaller businesses must also take seriously. For example, a large supermarket chain has already made an urgent request to all its Dutch suppliers to explain their cybersecurity policy, says Daan Hoogendijk, programme director of Samen Digitaal Veilig. “The message was: either you show a certificate, or we will end the cooperation. Unfortunately, most of these food companies still have too little focus on cybersecurity.” According to Hoogendijk, this is typical of the attitude of most SME companies.

text: The age-old question ‘what is there to get from my company?’ is no longer relevant.”. A survey by ABN AMRO and research agency MWM2 among 895 organisations shows that almost three-quarters (72 per cent) of respondents from large companies already question their own customers, suppliers and partners about this “very regularly” or “regularly”. Of them, 66 per cent say they also receive such questions from their chain partners.

Unfortunately, most of these food companies still have too little focus on cybersecurity.” According to Hoogendijk, this is typical of the attitude of most SME companies.

metadata.source_title: Cybersecurity for small businesses is crucial to retain customers
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Wet- en regelgeving
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: With NIS2 arriving, small businesses will face critical questions from the supply chain. This has not fully sunk in yet. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: They tend to wait and see, and other priorities block serious steps.

text: NIS2 comprises a set of rules primarily aimed at companies whose disruption can have a broad social impact. Broadly speaking, these are large and medium-sized companies with at least 50 employees or annual turnover and total assets of more than 10 million euros, and that are active in a sector of social importance.

metadata.source_title: Cybersecurity for small businesses is crucial to retain customers
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Wet- en regelgeving
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: With NIS2 arriving, small businesses will face critical questions from the supply chain. This has not fully sunk in yet. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: NIS2 comprises a set of rules primarily aimed at companies whose disruption can have a broad social impact. Broadly speaking, these are large and medium-sized companies with at least 50 employees or annual turnover and total assets of more than 10 million euros, and that are active in a sector of social importance. In total, this concerns 10,432 Dutch companies that must comply with the new rules, spread across seventeen sectors such as energy supply, digital infrastructure and government. The companies in scope are therefore expected to look critically at the cybersecurity of all their suppliers, including small ones.

text: NIS2 comprises a set of rules primarily aimed at companies whose disruption can have a broad social impact. Broadly speaking, these are large and medium-sized companies with at least 50 employees or annual turnover and total assets of more than 10 million euros, and that are active in a sector of social importance. In total, this concerns 10,432 Dutch companies that must comply with the new rules, spread across seventeen sectors such as energy supply, digital infrastructure and government.

metadata.parent_text: NIS2 comprises a set of rules primarily aimed at companies whose disruption can have a broad social impact. Broadly speaking, these are large and medium-sized companies with at least 50 employees or annual turnover and total assets of more than 10 million euros, and that are active in a sector of social importance. In total, this concerns 10,432 Dutch companies that must comply with the new rules, spread across seventeen sectors such as energy supply, digital infrastructure and government. The companies in scope are therefore expected to look critically at the cybersecurity of all their suppliers, including small ones.

text: Broadly speaking, these are large and medium-sized companies with at least 50 employees or annual turnover and total assets of more than 10 million euros, and that are active in a sector of social importance. The companies in scope are therefore expected to look critically at the cybersecurity of all their suppliers, including small ones.
